name: [Demo protocol] Growth-curve QC analysis
description: Demo script — loads the plate reader OD600 dump, fits exp. growth per well, and renders QC plots. Designed for the 96-well DemoStrain inducer titration (alex plate template id 7).
embedded_code:
"""Demo growth-curve QC script.

Reads a plate reader CSV (one row per well, one column per timepoint)
and fits an exponential growth model for the log-phase window.
"""
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# --- Config ---
INPUT_CSV = "demo-strain-inducer-titration.csv"  # exported from the plate reader
LOG_PHASE_HOURS = (2.0, 6.0)
BLANK_COLUMNS = ["A1"]  # see plate template (alex methods id 7)


def fit_exp_growth(times_h: np.ndarray, od: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """Linear fit on log(OD); returns specific growth rate (1/h)."""
    mask = (times_h >= LOG_PHASE_HOURS[0]) & (times_h <= LOG_PHASE_HOURS[1])
    slope, _ = np.polyfit(times_h[mask], np.log(od[mask]), 1)
    return float(slope)


def main() -> None:
    df = pd.read_csv(INPUT_CSV)
    time_h = df["time_h"].to_numpy()
    blanks = df[BLANK_COLUMNS].mean(axis=1).to_numpy()

    sample_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in ("time_h", *BLANK_COLUMNS)]
    rates = {}
    for well in sample_cols:
        od_corrected = df[well].to_numpy() - blanks
        rates[well] = fit_exp_growth(time_h, np.clip(od_corrected, 1e-3, None))

    rates_series = pd.Series(rates).sort_values(ascending=False)
    print("Top 8 wells by growth rate:")
    print(rates_series.head(8).to_string())

    # Quick visual — log-OD vs time, all wells.
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))
    for well in sample_cols:
        ax.plot(time_h, np.log(np.clip(df[well] - blanks, 1e-3, None)), alpha=0.4)
    ax.set_xlabel("Time (h)")
    ax.set_ylabel("log(OD600 - blank)")
    ax.set_title("Demo growth curves")
    fig.tight_layout()
    fig.savefig("growth-curves-qc.png", dpi=150)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
